title: A Musical
content:
Outside the rain upon the street,
    The sky all grim of hue,
    Inside, the music-painful sweet,
    And yet I heard but you.

    As is a thrilling violin,
    So is your voice to me,
    And still above the other strains,
    It sang in ecstasy.
meaning: About: Paul Laurence Dunbar was a seminal American poet of the late 19th and early 20th centuries. Dunbar gained national recognition for his 1896 Lyrics of a Lowly Life, one poem in the collection being Ode to Ethiopia.
